events.chronicle: In {year} a {neighborhood} repair shop started keeping hours for the hum. People brought radios that worked fine, and left them there, and stayed.
After the fire, a {neighborhood} survivor preached that the burn had been a first draft. By {year} the congregation carried matchbooks like rosaries.
Riders swear a train still runs the dead {neighborhood} line at 3:14 a.m. In {year} people began waiting for it with luggage.
events.news: GHOST TRAIN RUNS ON TIME, SAYS EVERYONE BUT THE MTA'S HEIRS

events.chronicle: A standpipe on #street# began running in {year} — tested, potable, unexplained. The line forms at dawn and nobody gets sick, which is the part nobody can explain.
A {neighborhood} man started reading pigeon flocks in {year} and called #augury_hit#. He takes no money, which everyone agrees makes it worse.
The {year} household survey included, without explanation, a question about dreams. #dream_census_result#

residue.grammar: a lineman's ladder painted gold and bolted to the wall, climbed by no one since {year}

events.chronicle: A lineman died bringing {neighborhood}'s power back in {year} and the block declined to move on. His ladder is painted gold. Outages route around the shrine, mostly.
Singing started in the dead tunnels under {neighborhood} in {year}. Acoustics, said the city. The acoustics take requests, said the neighbors.
Phones die on one block of #street#, reliably, since {year}. The carriers blame each other. The block holds its meetings there.
The {neighborhood} prophet stood trial for fraud in {year}. The prosecution proved the miracles were staged. Attendance doubled.
A date went around in {year} — chalked on walls, printed on cards, never sourced. Nothing happened on it. The faithful call that the strongest sign yet.
Someone took the #stolen_relic# from its {borough} shrine in {year}. Three crews claimed the job. Four returned it.
A {neighborhood} call center broke into tongues in {year}, all forty seats at once, mid-shift. The calls were recorded. The recordings disagree.
events.news: CALL CENTER SPEAKS IN TONGUES; HOLD TIMES UNAFFECTED

events.chronicle: An order of fasters took a {neighborhood} storefront in {year}. They eat nothing, on camera, and look wonderful. The waitlist runs two thousand names.

residue.grammar: a sidewalk altar with a framed photograph, fresh candles, and a petition clipboard, kept dry since {year}

events.chronicle: {neighborhood} petitioned Rome in {year} to open a cause for #saint_candidate#. Rome said such things take centuries. The block built the shrine anyway, to save time.
A {neighborhood} congregation started building a boat on a roof in {year}. They agree it will never touch water. That isn't what it's for.
By {year} a dead {borough} bookkeeper's margin notes were circulating as scripture. The doctrine is one line: everything is counted. The comfort and the terror are the same fact.
The {year} fog procession came back to {neighborhood} with #procession_oddity#.

residue.grammar: a rope of fresh flowers tied to a bell that is not there, renewed since {year}

events.chronicle: The bells of the deconsecrated church on #street# rang in {year}. The tower had been sealed; the bells had been sold. Both facts were checked that same morning.
Nobody preached it, but in {year} {borough} quietly stopped. Shrines went untended; pews thinned. Asked why, people mostly said it felt rude to keep asking for more.

fire_shrine: a ring of matchbooks under glass, one struck
char scraped into a spiral, renewed after every rain
a doorframe burned black and oiled like good furniture
augury_hit: the bridge closure to the day
dream_census_result: Eleven percent reported the door. The question was not repeated.
The results were sealed. The seal has a waiting list.
Two precincts answered identically, word for word.
The agency that asked has no record of asking.
silence_graffiti: LEAVE YOUR PHONE, TAKE THE QUIET
saint_candidate: a nurse who stayed through the water
the lineman of the long dark
a bodega keeper who fed three blocks through the Crash
a conductor who went back into the tunnel
ark_banner: DRY IS A PROMISE NOBODY KEPT
procession_oddity: more marchers than it left with, by every count but the official one
candles still lit that had been out at the turn
one fewer, and no one missing
tabloid_strange: HOLY HUM HAS THEM LINING UP
